# Verfix — Full Reference
> Local-first browser verification runtime for AI-generated web apps
> Version: 0.3.6
> License: Apache 2.0
> Docs: https://verfix.dev/docs
> Source: https://github.com/verfix-dev/verfix

---

## What is Verfix?

Verfix is a local-first browser verification runtime for AI coding agents. It gives agents
a deterministic environment to verify that UI changes actually work in a real browser —
entirely in-process on Node.js, no Docker or cloud account required.

Key properties:
- Runs locally in a single Node.js process — no Docker, no services, no cloud
- Playwright-powered browser execution; strict mode needs no AI key at all
- Structured JSON output designed for agent consumption
- Exit codes: 0 (pass), 1 (verification failure), 2 (setup error) — CI-friendly
- Every run records a full Playwright trace for human observability
- A git-baseline source guard flags/blocks agents rewriting app code to satisfy a broken selector

Architecture (default, local mode):
CLI (Node.js) → @verfix/engine (in-process, Playwright) → Browser → .verfix/runs/<id>.json + trace

Architecture (opt-in, --server, foundation of a future hosted CI product):
CLI → API (Go/Fiber) → Redis/BullMQ queue → Playwright Workers (container) → Postgres → Dashboard

---

## Loop Engineering: Verfix as the verifier

Loop engineering is the practice of designing the goal, verifier, state, stop condition, and
escalation rules that let an AI agent iterate safely — instead of prompting it turn by turn.
Verfix is the verifier half of that loop for UI code, so the model generating the code isn't also
grading its own work:

  Goal          → the flow — written by the agent from the app's real source, not a template
  Verifier      → typed assertions + an 8-value failure taxonomy — deterministic pass/fail
  State         → failures[], fix_hint, source_changes — carried between iterations as JSON
  Stop condition → exit codes 0 (pass) / 1 (verification failure) / 2 (setup error)
  Budget        → per-flow timeout/retries, plus an AI rate-limit circuit breaker
  Escalation    → after repeated failures, surface show_command to a human instead of looping

Agent-first, not agent-adjacent: the agent writes the flow, runs it, classifies the failure,
decides whether to fix the flow/config or the app (see Config-First Verification below), retries,
and escalates only when stuck.

---

## CLI Reference

### verfix init
Interactive setup wizard.
- Prompts: base URL, mode (default strict — no AI key needed), AI key (only if not strict)
- Makes sure Chromium is available (downloads once, ~130MB, cached)
- Writes verfix.config.json with an EMPTY flows: [] — no preset library, no scaffolding.
  Flows are written by the coding agent on demand, by reading the app source.
- Generates a compact AGENTS.md stub + full .verfix/INSTRUCTIONS.md reference
  (mirrors the stub into CLAUDE.md / .github/copilot-instructions.md / .clinerules/verfix.md when detected)
Options: --yes/-y (non-interactive), --ai-provider, --ai-model, --ai-key, --base-url, --mode,
--skip-runtime, --skip-agent-files, --dry-run, --force/-f, --server (opt-in Docker server mode)

### verfix run
Runs a verification job. In-process by default — no runtime to start first.
Options:
  --flow <id> / -f       Flow ID to run
  --url <url> / -u       Target URL
  --task <task> / -t     Task description (required for exploratory mode without flows)
  --mode <mode> / -m     strict | assisted | exploratory
  --output <format> / -o pretty | json (default: json)
  --config <file> / -c   Config file (default: ./verfix.config.json)
  --full                 Include the raw ExecutionResult (full event timeline) in JSON output
  --timeout <ms>         Timeout in ms (default: 15000)
  --retries <n>          Retries on failure (default: 2)
  --show-browser         Show the browser window (local mode)
  --source-policy <p>    warn | block | off (overrides config sourceCodePolicy)
  --reset-baseline       Reset the source-change baseline for this verify cycle
  --skip-download        Fail fast instead of auto-downloading Chromium
  --server               Run via the opt-in Docker server runtime

Exit codes: 0 = passed, 1 = verification failed, 2 = setup error

### verfix show [executionId]
Opens the recorded Playwright trace for a run (newest if no id given).
Options: --console (print captured console log), --network (print captured network requests),
--output pretty|json

### verfix probe [executionId]
Dry-run selectors/text against a run's saved end-of-run DOM snapshot (~1s vs a full run).
Options: --selector/-s <selectors...>, --text/-t <texts...>, --config/-c <file>, --output/-o
Exit 0 if every query matched, 1 if any missed.

### verfix validate
Checks verfix.config.json for structural/semantic errors without running anything.
Options: --config/-c <file>, --output/-o pretty|json

### verfix install
Downloads the Chromium browser separately from `verfix run` (one-time, ~130MB).

### verfix doctor
Runs diagnostic checks (local mode): Node version, engine installed, Chromium installed,
config valid, AGENTS.md present, app reachable, AI key/model valid if configured.
Docker is informational only in local mode. Exits with count of failed checks.

### verfix flows
Lists all flows defined in verfix.config.json.
Options: --config <file>, --output pretty|json

### verfix list
Lists recent local verification runs from .verfix/runs/.

### verfix status
Summarizes local setup: runner mode, config path, Chromium status, last run result.

### verfix start / stop / logs / update
No-ops in local mode (print what to do instead). Manage the opt-in Docker server runtime
with --server (or VERFIX_RUNNER=server env var).

---

## Output Contract

verfix run --output json returns (default, summarized):

{
  "passed": boolean,
  "failures": [
    {
      "type": "selector_not_found" | "selector_not_visible" | "text_mismatch" |
               "url_mismatch" | "console_error" | "network_failure" |
               "timeout" | "assertion_failed",
      "flow": string | undefined,
      "assertion": string | undefined,
      "selector": string | undefined,
      "detail": string | undefined,
      "fix_hint": string
    }
  ],
  "timeline_url": string | null,   // null in local mode; populated only in --server mode
  "trace_path": ".verfix/runs/exec_xxx_trace.zip",
  "show_command": "verfix show exec_xxx",
  "detail_commands": { "console": "...", "network": "..." },
  "duration_ms": number,
  "retry_count": number,
  "exit_code": 0 | 1,
  "execution_id": "exec_xxx",
  "skipped_optional_steps": [ /* present if any optional step was skipped */ ],
  "source_changes": { /* present when project/config files changed during the verify loop */ }
}

Pass --full to get the complete raw ExecutionResult (full event timeline) instead of the summary.
The summary is the default because the full timeline was ~93% of the payload size and agents paid
for it on every fix-loop iteration; nothing non-nominal is silent in the summary.

---

## Failure Taxonomy

selector_not_found
  - Meaning: CSS/testId selector matched zero elements in DOM
  - Fix: Reuse the element's real selector from source (directly or via the `selectors` alias
    map); add a `wait_for_selector` step if it renders after an async operation. Add a new
    data-testid only as a last resort — see Config-First Verification below.

selector_not_visible
  - Meaning: Element exists but is hidden (display:none, visibility:hidden, zero size)
  - Fix: Check conditional rendering; ensure trigger step runs before assertion

text_mismatch
  - Meaning: Expected text was not found on the page
  - Fix: Update assertion value; add wait step for async data to load

url_mismatch
  - Meaning: Current URL does not contain the expected substring
  - Fix: Check routing configuration; verify prior flow steps succeeded

console_error
  - Meaning: One or more console.error() calls detected during flow
  - Fix: Fix the JS error in your application; check the console log via `verfix show --console`

network_failure
  - Meaning: A required network request returned non-2xx status
  - Fix: Ensure backend is running; check network requests via `verfix show --network`; consider
    `acceptStatuses` on the assertion if more than one status is a valid outcome

timeout
  - Meaning: An operation exceeded configured timeout (default: 15000ms)
  - Fix: Increase timeout in config or step; fix slow page load

assertion_failed
  - Meaning: Generic fallback — no more specific type applies
  - Fix: Read the detail field; open the trace with `show_command` for full replay

Exit code 2 (setup error, e.g. bad config, unknown flow, missing ${VAR}) is distinct from exit
code 1 (a real verification failure) — check which one you got before treating it as an app bug.

---

## Config Schema (verfix.config.json)

Top-level:
  baseUrl: string (required) — app base URL, supports ${VAR} interpolation
  mode: "strict" | "assisted" | "exploratory" (default: "strict")
  task: string (optional) — natural language task for exploratory mode
  flows: Flow[] (required)
  assertions: AssertionDefinition[] (optional) — global assertions
  selectors: Record<string, string> (optional) — alias map, core to config-first targeting
  sourceCodePolicy: "warn" | "block" | "off" (default: "warn")
  browser: { channel?: string, headless?: boolean } (optional)
  metadata: object (optional)
  timeout: number (optional, default: 15000ms)
  retries: number (optional, default: 2)

Flow:
  id: string (required)
  name: string (optional)
  mode: "strict" | "assisted" (optional) — overrides global mode (not "exploratory" per-flow)
  steps: FlowStep[] (required)
  assertions: AssertionDefinition[] (optional)
  clearState: boolean (optional) — clear cookies/storage before this flow runs
  saveState: string (optional) — persist auth state under this name after pass
  useState: string (optional) — restore named auth state before running
  skip: boolean (optional) — quarantine flow from full runs
  skipReason: string (optional)

FlowStep actions:
  navigate            — { action, url }
  click                — { action, selector?/testId?/text?, timeout? }
  type                 — { action, selector?/testId?/text?, value, timeout? }
  press                — { action, key, selector?/testId?/text? }
  select_option        — { action, selector, value }
  check / uncheck      — { action, selector }
  hover                — { action, selector }
  upload_file          — { action, selector, file: string | { name, content, mimeType, encoding? } }
  wait_for_selector    — { action, selector?/testId?, timeout? }
  wait_for_url         — { action, value }
  wait_for_network_idle — { action }
  Any step also accepts: optional?: boolean, frame?: string (iframe selector)

AssertionDefinition types:
  page_loaded             — no extra fields required
  selector_visible         — selector: string, timeout?: number
  text_visible             — value: string, selector?: string (scopes the search), timeout?: number
  url_contains             — value: string
  title_contains           — value: string
  no_console_errors        — exclude?: string[] (regex patterns to ignore)
  network_request_success  — value: string (URL pattern), acceptStatuses?: number[]

${VAR} interpolation: step value/url, assertion value, and baseUrl may reference ${VAR_NAME},
resolved from process.env (incl. .verfix/.env) at run time. Unset var fails the run immediately.

---

## Execution Modes

strict (default):
  Selector strategy: data-testid → CSS only
  AI usage: None — needs no AI key
  Reliability: 5/5
  Best for: CI/CD, regression testing

assisted:
  Selector strategy: data-testid → semantic → AI fallback
  AI usage: Recovery only (when selector fails). Works without a key — semantic tier only.
  Recovery chain: exact → semantic (aria-label, role, text) → AI (LLM DOM analysis)
  Reliability: 4/5
  Best for: Active development, evolving UIs

exploratory:
  Selector strategy: AI-planned
  AI usage: Core driver — requires an AI key, fails fast with ai_key_required if missing
  Reliability: 2/5
  Best for: Discovery, ad-hoc QA, unknown flows

---

## Config-First Verification (important for agents)

When a selector fails, agents should fix it in Verfix configuration — not by editing app source
to make a test pass. Priority order: (1) reuse the element's existing selector from source,
directly or via the `selectors` alias map — deterministic, works in strict mode; (2) a semantic
selector (role/text); (3) let `assisted` mode self-heal as a resilience fallback, not a targeting
strategy; (4) add a new data-testid to source only as a last resort.

Enforcement: `verfix run` snapshots a git baseline at the start of each verify cycle and reports
a `source_changes` field naming project files edited since the baseline, classified `project` vs
`config`. `sourceCodePolicy` controls enforcement: `warn` (default, non-blocking) / `block` (fails
the run with `source_edit_blocked` until reverted) / `off`. Flags: --source-policy, --reset-baseline.
Full guide: https://verfix.dev/config-first-verification

---

## Agent Retry Loop

Pseudocode (TypeScript):

async function verifyAndFix(flowId: string, maxRetries = 3) {
  for (let attempt = 0; attempt < maxRetries; attempt++) {
    const { stdout } = await exec(`verfix run --flow ${flowId} --output json`);
    const result = JSON.parse(stdout);

    if (result.passed) return; // verified

    for (const failure of result.failures) {
      // failure.type tells you what broke; failure.flow/assertion tell you where
      // failure.fix_hint tells you exactly how to fix it — fix the flow/config first
      await applyFix(failure);
    }

    if (attempt === maxRetries - 1) {
      // Surface to human
      throw new Error(`Open trace: ${result.show_command}`);
    }
  }
}

---

## Self-Hosting (opt-in server mode, --server)

Not required by default. Docker-based API + queue + timeline dashboard — the foundation of a
future hosted CI product.

docker run -d \
  --name verfix \
  --network=host \
  -e VERFIX_HOST_NETWORK=1 \
  -e OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... \
  -v verfix-data:/var/lib/postgresql/15/main \
  -v verfix-artifacts:/app/workers/artifacts \
  ghcr.io/verfix-dev/verfix-server:latest

Health check: GET http://localhost:3611/api/v1/health → { "status": "healthy" }

Environment variables:
  OPENAI_API_KEY / provider key  — Required for assisted/exploratory modes
  AI_MODEL                        — Provider default if unset
  MAX_CONCURRENCY                 — Default: 3

Volumes:
  verfix-data       — PostgreSQL data (execution history)
  verfix-artifacts  — Screenshots, traces, HAR files

Ports:
  3610 — Dashboard (execution timeline UI)
  3611 — API (Go/Fiber REST)

Full guide: https://verfix.dev/self-hosting

---

## AGENTS.md

verfix init writes a compact AGENTS.md stub (~30 lines: identity, the config-first rule, core
commands) plus the full reference at .verfix/INSTRUCTIONS.md (config schema, step/assertion
vocabulary, failure taxonomy, output contract, flow-writing guide). Read natively by Codex,
Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Kilo, opencode, Zed, Jules, and 20+ other agents. For tools that don't
read AGENTS.md natively, the same stub is mirrored into CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md,
or .clinerules/verfix.md when detected.
